title: Majana canina
taxonomy: Majana canina, also known as Mexican heather or False Heather, belongs to the family Verbenaceae. This plant is classified as a perennial shrub and is native to Mexico and Central America. The scientific name for Majana canina is also known as Pentas lanceolata and Lippia alba, however Lippia alba is another species,  it is more commonly known as  Lippia nodiflora.  Lippia is the Genus of the Majana canina.
uses: Majana canina, commonly known as coyote mint, has been used in traditional medicine for its various benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. It is also used to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, due to its carminative properties. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat fever and headaches, as well as to reduce stress and anxiety. Its medicinal uses have been documented in various traditional remedies across North America.
